methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
ISEE, a Series B drone and autonomous systems company founded by Yibiao Zhao, has raised $70M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector includes eight private peers—among them Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Archer Aviation—but no public comparables. A narrower peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within the private set; only one direct Series B comparable exists in the filtered universe.

Stage-progression methodology was therefore applied as the primary valuation anchor. Using total capital raised ($70M) as a base proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, this method yields an implied valuation of $133M. This represents a reasonable progression from the company's historical funding trajectory and is consistent with typical venture capital round advancement patterns in the autonomous systems space.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $113M to $153M, centered on $133M. The wide band reflects reliance on a single triangulation method and the absence of granular peer benchmarking within ISEE's immediate cohort. Key uncertainties include the actual revenue run rate, competitive positioning relative to the named peers, and market timing within a sector experiencing significant public-market volatility. Additional revenue or unit economics data would narrow this range materially.
